name: True Garlic Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 Garlic Puree (2 Roasted Heads)        2 tablespoons Kosher Salt
1/4 pound Unsalted Butter, Softened       3 1/4 cups Whole Wheat Flour
2 teaspoons (2 packets) Dry Yeast		 3 1/4 cups Unbleached All Purpose Flour
1/2 cup Warm Water (115-120 degrees)        1 x Cornmeal
recipeInstructions:
1. Cream together the garlic puree and butter. This can be done days in advance and refrigerated; bring to room temperature before using.
2. In a large bowl, combine yeast with half a cup warm water. Stir with a fork or small whisk. Add an additional 2 1/2 cups water. Add salt. Stir in the flour, one cup at a time, starting with whole wheat. Use a whisk until the dough is stiff, then switch to a wooden spoon.
3. Turn the dough onto a well-floured work surface. Knead rhythmically for 10 to 15 minutes, until the dough is smooth, springy, non-sticky, and elastic. Add more flour as needed while kneading. The dough is ready when indentations from your fingers spring back.
4. Cover the dough with a cloth and let rest while you wash, dry, and generously butter the bowl. Knead the dough a few more turns, then form it into a ball and place in the bowl. Turn to coat with butter. Cover the bowl and put it in a warm, draft-free place until the dough has doubled in bulk, about 1 1/2 hours.
5. When doubled, flour your fist and gently punch down the dough. Knead it a few times and let rest.
6. Sprinkle baking sheets generously with cornmeal. Divide dough into three equal parts. Keep two covered while working on one piece. Flour your work surface. Roll each piece of dough into a rectangle approximately 14 inches long by 7 inches wide using a rolling pin.
7. Spread softened garlic butter over the dough. Roll the long edge toward the opposite long edge, as if rolling up a rug. Pinch ends closed to form rolls. Place on baking sheets. With a sharp knife or razor blade, make light slashes at two-inch intervals across each roll.
8. Cover with cloth and place in a warm, draft-free area to rise until doubled again, about 1/2 hour. Meanwhile, preheat oven to 400 degrees F (200 degrees C).
9. Bake for 35 to 40 minutes with a pan of boiling water on the oven floor. Spray loaves with water several times during baking to achieve a thick crusty shell.
10. To test for doneness, rap the loaf with your knuckles; it should sound hollow. Cool on wire racks, but the loaves are delicious eaten warm right out of the oven.
